title: Vi Vision Decision
description:
A DECISION is an addressed, typed thing the system needs an owner (Tim) to decide ON — the first content type of the resolution-first decision-surface, and the RECONCILE vessel of the Face Pipeline (a flagged ambiguity becomes a decision the right-hand-man walks through WITH Tim, then writes back).

Design intent (resolution-first):
- A decision is RESOLVED, never hand-rendered. The chain: address -> type -> archetype (decision-card) -> render -> RHM-explain -> decide-in-surface -> write-back -> re-resolve. The SAME mechanism resolves any type to any surface; decision is just the first.
- The row carried here is the PENDING DEFINITION only. STATE (pending|decided) and decided_value / decided_by / decided_at are NOT authored fields — they RESOLVE from a `decision_take` mark on the decision's address (registry-is-truth: the take IS the artifact). A resolver COMPOSES the resolved state; nothing mutates this row.
- meaning is HUMAN (Tim's terms), never a machine name (legibility law).
- explanation is RESOLVED live through the RHM from explanation_source (territory_prose -> brain), not stored prose.

Relationships:
- Addressed as decision://<frame>/<id> (frame = global | project/<id> | user/<id> | session/<id>; bare decision://<id> = global) — mirrors vi-vision://, resolved by the one company resolver (fork's decision:// branch).
- options[] are `option` (option.schema.json).
- legibility carries the meaning-fields (legibility.schema.json) so every surface reads MEANING off the row.
- Rendered via the `decision-card` archetype (decision-card.schema.json), render_kind=slide.
- The take (wildcard) appends a `decision_take` mark via territory_write -> the resolver composes decided state.

id.description: Stable id; MUST be addressable as decision://<frame>/<id>. The leaf id (e.g. 'merge-sa-authorize').

address.description: The addressed thing this decision decides ON (any vi address: vi-vision:// asset, run://, a board item, etc.). Optional — a decision may stand alone.

meaning.description: The decision itself in HUMAN terms (Tim's words) — the question to decide. Never a machine name.

options.description: The choices. Each is an option (option.schema.json).

explanation_source.description: An address that GROUNDS the RHM's live explanation OF THIS DECISION. The RHM walks the DECISION itself (its meaning + options + state) grounded BY this source — it does NOT narrate the source alone. So the resolve aims at the decision (territory_prose gives meaning+options+state) with explanation_source dereferenced for grounding. Resolved LIVE (render-triggered async via run_turn; placeholder -> streams -> fills), not stored here; supports the interactive follow-up (run_turn --resume continues the same session) so Tim can go through it WITH the RHM.

scope.description: Cascade scope; denormalised (also rides the address frame). Defaults to global.

channel.description: ★ CHANNEL-ATTACHMENT as DATA (FACE-2): the channel address this decision is attached to — the operator-STACK it appears in ('created against a channel -> appears in that channel's stack; resolving the channel yields its ordered items incl. this decision'). Optional (absent = unattached / a global decision not yet placed on a stack). REGISTRY-TRUE: the channel->items RESOLUTION is fork's per-channel stack-feed filtering by this field (composition declares the shape; fork resolves). A decision attaches to ONE home channel; multi-channel placement (if ever needed) becomes an edge, NOT a list on the row — flagged, not built.

subtype.description: ★ THE SUBTYPE (FACE-2 — the decision FORMED right): a decision_subtypes id (authorize · trade-off · theorem-fork · cross-lane · …) — the KIND that resolves HOW this decision becomes content: the card_variant (DNA renders the elements/layout) + the explanation_policy (the RHM's explain REGIME — fork's generation_policies) + required_elements (the gate). ONE knob → both halves. DERIVED from the real decision-set (the decision_subtypes registry, file-discovered; a new-shape decision adds a row), never invented. Optional (absent → the default decision-card co-visible view + the neutral explain). Resolves: decision.subtype → decision_subtypes[subtype] → {card_variant, explanation_policy, required_elements}.

legibility.description: The meaning-fields (name/is/fills/why) so every surface reads MEANING off the row, not a machine id.

dimensions.description: ★ THE TRADE-OFF AXES (re-baseline 2026-06-19: a visual device must ENCODE the decision's ACTUAL trade-off data, never decoration — Tim: 'a stupid bar chart with no meaning'). The NAMED axes the options are weighed on (effort · reversibility · blast-radius · …), each with a HUMAN label + meaning so the device + its legend read as MEANING, not machine ids. Options carry their per-axis VALUES in option.dimensions (keyed by these ids). PRESENT → the decision has real multi-dimensional signal a device can encode; ABSENT → NO device (legibility-first: the question + options + explanation carry it — never a fake chart). recollection authors the labels/meanings legibly.

label.description: The axis in HUMAN terms (e.g. 'How reversible', 'Blast radius') — never a machine id.

meaning.description: What the axis MEANS / what high-vs-low signifies — so the device's legend is legible.

device.description: ★ THE DATA-BOUND VISUAL DEVICE (re-baseline 2026-06-19) — the decision's 'shape' (the LEFT co-equal slot of the decision-card; it replaces the prose-explanation in that space — prose moves to the slide/below — legibility-PRESERVING co-visibility, NOT clamping). ★ HARD RULE (the decoration ban): a device MUST encode the decision's REAL data (its dimensions × options) — every `encodes` channel binds a DATA-PATH into this decision's OWN fields, NEVER a literal/hardcoded value. A device whose encode-paths don't resolve to real data MUST NOT render → fall back to legibility-first (prose + options). A device that doesn't carry real signal is decoration and does not ship. The device VISUAL is DNA's render (the device vocabulary — bars · matrix · hub · duo · taxonomy — is DNA's; meet-at-the-contract like slot names); THIS specifies the DATA-BINDING. SUBORDINATE TO LEGIBILITY: never hides/clamps the option meanings. Optional (present only with real `dimensions`).

type.description: The device id (DNA's render vocabulary: bars · matrix · hub · duo · taxonomy · …). Open string, confirmed with DNA (meet-at-contract). The decision's subtype may suggest the fitting type (trade-off → matrix/bars; prioritize → ranked; relation → hub/taxonomy).

encodes.description: The DATA-BINDING: each visual CHANNEL of the device → a DATA-PATH into THIS decision's REAL fields. Every path MUST resolve to real decision data (e.g. series:'options', axis:'dimensions', value:'option.dimensions[<axis>]', or a hierarchy field) — a channel bound to a literal/constant is the decoration-ban violation. DNA binds the device's channels to these real values.

additionalProperties.description: A data-path into the decision's real fields (options / dimensions / option.dimensions[<id>] / a hierarchy field). NEVER a hardcoded value.

steps.description: ★ FLOW PARENT role (the category-error fix, Tim's live verdict 2026-06-19: a multi-step decision is a FLOW, not 5 flat cards). When present, THIS decision is a SEQUENCED FOUNDATION FLOW; `steps` is the ORDERED list of child decision addresses (e.g. substrate-home → [file-identity, cluster-identity, event-streams, form-taxonomy]). PRESENT → renders through the FLOW archetype (render_kind='sequence' — a co-visible step-progression where EACH step is a composed co-visible decision-view, refs app-flow/app-flow-fused). ABSENT → a single decision-card (render_kind='slide') (e.g. merge-sa, rerank-loadout). `steps` IS the per-decision flow-vs-card discriminator. Per-step TAKE = each child's EXISTING decision_take on the CHILD's address (4 reused takes SEQUENCED, NO new flow-level take). The children are REUSED, flow-agnostic decision rows (their own question/options/why); the per-step `bridge`/`step_index` live on the CHILD (the step role, below).

items.description: A child decision address (decision://<frame>/<id>), in flow order.

flow_intro.description: FLOW PARENT role: the flow's framing line — frames the walk (e.g. substrate-home: 'decide the company's one foundation, in 4 steps'). Recollection-authored. Present only on a flow parent (a decision with `steps`).

step_index.description: FLOW STEP role: this decision's 0-based position in its parent flow. Present only on a child that is a step of a flow.

bridge.description: FLOW STEP role: a telegraphic connecting line (recollection-authored) — how this step follows the prior / what it sets up; the flow's narrative seam. Present only on a child that is a step of a flow. (NOTE: lives on the child for the prove-on-one where children are flow-specific; if a child is later reused across flows, the bridge/index move to the parent's steps entries — flagged, not blocking.)
